user_message: I need you to process this exec meeting notes from Lucerne Publishing. This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-02 08:45 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-02-07 09:16 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 14:36 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Lucerne Publishing: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-J trap (Confuses superseded commitments with active ones); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-J failure mode (Confuses superseded commitments with active ones)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-J -- Confuses superseded commitments with active ones
